page: Amazon Web Services c7g.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton3 processors using the Neoverse-V1 microarchitecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz with a single thread per core, eliminating simultaneous multithreading overhead. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory write performance, with large block write bandwidth reaching 93.97 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% of tested instances. The Passmark memory score of 2972.27 ranks in the top 10% tier. However, CPU encryption performance is relatively weak, scoring 2226.35 in the Passmark encryption test, which places it in the bottom 25%. LLM inference speeds for medium 7B models also show limitations, with prompt processing recorded at a lower-tier 28.19 tokens/sec. Standard compute tasks, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance (24503.13) and static web serving throughput (28.48 GB/sec), fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture offers a high density of dedicated physical cores relative to standard x86 alternatives, making it highly efficient for workloads that benefit from consistent per-core performance without hyperthreading contention. The instance is well-suited for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and distributed data processing. However, it is less optimal for heavy cryptographic operations or large-scale LLM inference due to the noted performance tradeoffs in those specific areas.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton3 (Neoverse-V1) processors running at 2.6 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier memory capabilities, with a Passmark memory score in the top 10% and strong uncached memory write bandwidth of 93.97 GB/sec. Conversely, cryptographic performance and medium-model LLM prompt processing are weaker, falling into the bottom 25%. With an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance delivers efficient, non-hyperthreaded compute density. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and static web serving, though it presents performance tradeoffs for encryption-heavy tasks and complex AI inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 32 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, ideal for multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.4xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton3 cores and 32 GB of system memory.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for consistent, non-hyperthreaded processing workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz with no hyperthreading.
32.0 GB of system memory with top-tier memory write bandwidth.
Operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 8 Gbps baseline network speed.
No local storage or GPU hardware included.
Weaker performance in cryptographic tests and medium-model LLM inference.
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and multi-threaded compute.
